completions.label: ActivateAdHocSubProcessActivitiesAsync
completions.documentation: Activate activities within an ad-hoc sub-process Activates selected activities within an ad-hoc sub-process identified by element ID. The provided element IDs must exist within the ad-hoc sub-process instance identified by the provided adHocSubProcessInstanceKey.

completions.label: ActivatedJob
completions.documentation: An activated job received from the Camunda broker, with typed variable access. This is what job handler functions receive.

completions.label: ActivateJobsAsync
completions.documentation: Activate jobs Iterate through all known partitions and activate jobs up to the requested maximum.

completions.label: AssignClientToGroupAsync

completions.label: AssignClientToTenantAsync
completions.documentation: Assign a client to a tenant Assign the client to the specified tenant. The client can then access tenant data and perform authorized actions.

completions.label: AssignGroupToTenantAsync
completions.documentation: Assign a group to a tenant Assigns a group to a specified tenant. Group members (users, clients) can then access tenant data and perform authorized actions.

completions.label: AssignMappingRuleToGroupAsync

completions.label: AssignMappingRuleToTenantAsync
completions.documentation: Assign a mapping rule to a tenant Assign a single mapping rule to a specified tenant.

completions.label: AssignRoleToClientAsync
completions.documentation: Assign a role to a client Assigns the specified role to the client. The client will inherit the authorizations associated with this role.

completions.label: AssignRoleToGroupAsync
completions.documentation: Assign a role to a group Assigns the specified role to the group. Every member of the group (user or client) will inherit the authorizations associated with this role.

completions.label: AssignRoleToMappingRuleAsync
completions.documentation: Assign a role to a mapping rule Assigns a role to a mapping rule.

completions.label: AssignRoleToTenantAsync
completions.documentation: Assign a role to a tenant Assigns a role to a specified tenant. Users, Clients or Groups, that have the role assigned, will get access to the tenant's data and can perform actions according to their authorizations.

completions.label: AssignRoleToUserAsync
completions.documentation: Assign a role to a user Assigns the specified role to the user. The user will inherit the authorizations associated with this role.

completions.label: AssignUserTaskAsync
completions.documentation: Assign user task Assigns a user task with the given key to the given assignee. Assignment waits for blocking task listeners on this lifecycle transition. If listener processing is delayed beyond the request timeout, this endpoint can return 504. Other gateway timeout causes are also possible. Retry with backoff and inspect listener worker availability and logs when this repeats.

completions.label: AssignUserToGroupAsync
completions.documentation: Assign a user to a group Assigns a user to a group, making the user a member of the group. Group members inherit the group authorizations, roles, and tenant assignments.

completions.label: AssignUserToTenantAsync
completions.documentation: Assign a user to a tenant Assign a single user to a specified tenant. The user can then access tenant data and perform authorized actions.

completions.label: AssumeExists

completions.label: AutoStart
completions.documentation: Whether to start polling immediately on creation. Default: true .

completions.label: AwaitCompletion
completions.documentation: Wait for the process instance to complete. If the process instance does not complete within the request timeout limit, a 504 response status will be returned. The process instance will continue to run in the background regardless of the timeout. Disabled by default.

completions.label: BroadcastSignalAsync
completions.documentation: Broadcast signal Broadcasts a signal.

completions.label: Brokers
completions.documentation: A list of brokers that are part of this cluster.

completions.label: BusinessId
completions.documentation: An optional, user-defined string identifier that identifies the process instance within the scope of a process definition (scoped by tenant). If provided and uniqueness enforcement is enabled, the engine will reject creation if another root process instance with the same business id is already active for the same process definition. Note that any active child process instances with the same business id are not taken into account.

completions.label: CamundaClient
completions.documentation: Primary Camunda client. Provides typed methods for all Camunda 8 REST API operations. Auto-generated operation methods are added in the Generated/ partial class files. This class provides the infrastructure: configuration, auth, retry, backpressure.

completions.label: CamundaClient
completions.documentation: Create a new CamundaClient with the given options.

completions.label: CamundaOptions
completions.documentation: Options for constructing a CamundaClient. Mirrors the JS SDK's CamundaOptions with idiomatic C# conventions.

completions.label: CancelBatchOperationAsync
completions.documentation: Cancel Batch operation Cancels a running batch operation. This is done asynchronously, the progress can be tracked using the batch operation status endpoint (/batch-operations/{batchOperationKey}).

completions.label: CancelProcessInstanceAsync
completions.documentation: Cancel process instance Cancels a running process instance. As a cancellation includes more than just the removal of the process instance resource, the cancellation resource must be posted. Cancellation can wait on listener-related processing; when that processing does not complete in time, this endpoint can return 504. Other gateway timeout causes are also possible. Retry with backoff and inspect listener worker availability and logs when this repeats.

completions.label: CancelProcessInstancesBatchOperationAsync
completions.documentation: Cancel process instances (batch) Cancels multiple running process instances. Since only ACTIVE root instances can be cancelled, any given filters for state and parentProcessInstanceKey are ignored and overridden during this batch operation. This is done asynchronously, the progress can be tracked using the batchOperationKey from the response and the batch operation status endpoint (/batch-operations/{batchOperationKey}).

completions.label: ClusterId
completions.documentation: The cluster Id.

completions.label: ClusterSize
completions.documentation: The number of brokers in the cluster.

completions.label: CompleteJobAsync
completions.documentation: Complete job Complete a job with the given payload, which allows completing the associated service task.

completions.label: CompleteUserTaskAsync
completions.documentation: Complete user task Completes a user task with the given key. Completion waits for blocking task listeners on this lifecycle transition. If listener processing is delayed beyond the request timeout, this endpoint can return 504. Other gateway timeout causes are also possible. Retry with backoff and inspect listener worker availability and logs when this repeats.

completions.label: Config
completions.documentation: The current hydrated configuration (read-only).

completions.label: Configuration
completions.documentation: An IConfiguration section (typically configuration.GetSection("Camunda") ) to bind settings from appsettings.json or any other configuration provider. Keys use PascalCase property names (e.g. RestAddress , Auth:Strategy ) and are mapped to the canonical CAMUNDA_* env-var names internally. Precedence (highest wins): Config > Configuration > environment variables > defaults.

completions.label: CorrelateMessageAsync
completions.documentation: Correlate message Publishes a message and correlates it to a subscription. If correlation is successful it will return the first process instance key the message correlated with. The message is not buffered. Use the publish message endpoint to send messages that can be buffered.

completions.label: Create
completions.documentation: Create a new CamundaClient.

completions.label: CreateAdminUserAsync
completions.documentation: Create admin user Creates a new user and assigns the admin role to it. This endpoint is only usable when users are managed in the Orchestration Cluster and while no user is assigned to the admin role.

completions.label: CreateAuthorizationAsync
completions.documentation: Create authorization Create the authorization.

completions.label: CreateDeploymentAsync
completions.documentation: Deploy resources Deploys one or more resources (e.g. processes, decision models, or forms). This is an atomic call, i.e. either all resources are deployed or none of them are.

completions.label: CreateDocumentAsync
completions.documentation: Upload document Upload a document to the Camunda 8 cluster. Note that this is currently supported for document stores of type: AWS, GCP, in-memory (non-production), local (non-production)

completions.label: CreateDocumentLinkAsync
completions.documentation: Create document link Create a link to a document in the Camunda 8 cluster. Note that this is currently supported for document stores of type: AWS, GCP

completions.label: CreateDocumentsAsync
completions.documentation: Upload multiple documents Upload multiple documents to the Camunda 8 cluster. The caller must provide a file name for each document, which will be used in case of a multi-status response to identify which documents failed to upload. The file name can be provided in the `Content-Disposition` header of the file part or in the `fileName` field of the metadata. You can add a parallel array of metadata objects. These are matched with the files based on index, and must have the same length as the files array. To pass homogenous metadata for all files, spread the metadata over the metadata array. A filename value provided explicitly via the metadata array in the request overrides the `Content-Disposition` header of the file part. In case of a multi-status response, the response body will contain a list of `DocumentBatchProblemDetail` objects, each of which contains the file name of the document that failed to upload and the reason for the failure. The client can choose to retry the whole batch or individual documents based on the response. Note that this is currently supported for document stores of type: AWS, GCP, in-memory (non-production), local (non-production)

completions.label: CreateElementInstanceVariablesAsync
completions.documentation: Update element instance variables Updates all the variables of a particular scope (for example, process instance, element instance) with the given variable data. Specify the element instance in the `elementInstanceKey` parameter. Variable updates can be delayed by listener-related processing; if processing exceeds the request timeout, this endpoint can return 504. Other gateway timeout causes are also possible. Retry with backoff and inspect listener worker availability and logs when this repeats.

completions.label: CreateGlobalClusterVariableAsync
completions.documentation: Create a global-scoped cluster variable Create a global-scoped cluster variable.

completions.label: CreateGlobalTaskListenerAsync

completions.label: CreateGroupAsync
completions.documentation: Create group Create a new group.

completions.label: CreateJobWorker
completions.documentation: Create a job worker that polls for and processes jobs of the specified type. The handler receives an ActivatedJob and returns variables to auto-complete. Throw BpmnErrorException for BPMN errors, JobFailureException for explicit failures, or any other exception to auto-fail with retries - 1 .

completions.label: CreateMappingRuleAsync
completions.documentation: Create mapping rule Create a new mapping rule

completions.label: CreateProcessInstanceAsync
completions.documentation: Create process instance Creates and starts an instance of the specified process. The process definition to use to create the instance can be specified either using its unique key (as returned by Deploy resources), or using the BPMN process id and a version. Waits for the completion of the process instance before returning a result when awaitCompletion is enabled.

completions.label: DeleteDocumentAsync
completions.documentation: Delete document Delete a document from the Camunda 8 cluster. Note that this is currently supported for document stores of type: AWS, GCP, in-memory (non-production), local (non-production)

completions.label: DeleteGlobalClusterVariableAsync
completions.documentation: Delete a global-scoped cluster variable Delete a global-scoped cluster variable.

completions.label: DeleteProcessInstanceAsync
completions.documentation: Delete process instance Deletes a process instance. Only instances that are completed or terminated can be deleted.

completions.label: DeleteProcessInstancesBatchOperationAsync
completions.documentation: Delete process instances (batch) Delete multiple process instances. This will delete the historic data from secondary storage. Only process instances in a final state (COMPLETED or TERMINATED) can be deleted. This is done asynchronously, the progress can be tracked using the batchOperationKey from the response and the batch operation status endpoint (/batch-operations/{batchOperationKey}).

completions.label: DeleteResourceAsync
completions.documentation: Delete resource Deletes a deployed resource. This can be a process definition, decision requirements definition, or form definition deployed using the deploy resources endpoint. Specify the resource you want to delete in the `resourceKey` parameter. Once a resource has been deleted it cannot be recovered. If the resource needs to be available again, a new deployment of the resource is required. By default, only the resource itself is deleted from the runtime state. To also delete the historic data associated with a resource, set the `deleteHistory` flag in the request body to `true`. The historic data is deleted asynchronously via a batch operation. The details of the created batch operation are included in the response. Note that history deletion is only supported for process resources; for other resource types this flag is ignored and no history will be deleted.

completions.label: DeleteTenantAsync

completions.label: JobKey
completions.documentation: Unique identifier for this job.

completions.label: JobTimeoutMs
completions.documentation: How long (in ms) the job is reserved for this worker before the broker makes it available to other workers. Falls back to CAMUNDA_WORKER_TIMEOUT environment variable.

completions.label: JobType
completions.documentation: The BPMN job type to subscribe to (e.g. "payment-service" ).

completions.label: JobWorkerConfig
completions.documentation: Configuration for a JobWorker.

completions.label: Kind
completions.documentation: The job kind.

completions.label: LastCompletedChangeId
completions.documentation: ID of the last completed change

completions.label: ListenerEventType
completions.documentation: The listener event type.

completions.label: LoggerFactory
completions.documentation: Logger factory for SDK logging.

completions.label: MaxConcurrentJobs
completions.documentation: Maximum number of jobs that may be in-flight (activated and being handled) concurrently by this worker. Controls how many jobs are requested per poll and how many handler tasks run in parallel. For I/O-bound handlers (HTTP calls, database queries), higher values (32–128) improve throughput because async handlers release threads during awaits. For CPU-bound handlers, set to ProcessorCount or lower to avoid over-subscribing the thread pool. Set to 1 for sequential (single-job-at-a-time) processing. Falls back to CAMUNDA_WORKER_MAX_CONCURRENT_JOBS environment variable, then 10 .

completions.label: MigrateProcessInstanceAsync
completions.documentation: Migrate process instance Migrates a process instance to a new process definition. This request can contain multiple mapping instructions to define mapping between the active process instance's elements and target process definition elements. Use this to upgrade a process instance to a new version of a process or to a different process definition, e.g. to keep your running instances up-to-date with the latest process improvements.

completions.label: MigrateProcessInstancesBatchOperationAsync
completions.documentation: Migrate process instances (batch) Migrate multiple process instances. Since only process instances with ACTIVE state can be migrated, any given filters for state are ignored and overridden during this batch operation. This is done asynchronously, the progress can be tracked using the batchOperationKey from the response and the batch operation status endpoint (/batch-operations/{batchOperationKey}).

completions.label: ModifyProcessInstanceAsync
completions.documentation: Modify process instance Modifies a running process instance. This request can contain multiple instructions to activate an element of the process or to terminate an active instance of an element. Use this to repair a process instance that is stuck on an element or took an unintended path. For example, because an external system is not available or doesn't respond as expected.

completions.label: ModifyProcessInstancesBatchOperationAsync
completions.documentation: Modify process instances (batch) Modify multiple process instances. Since only process instances with ACTIVE state can be modified, any given filters for state are ignored and overridden during this batch operation. In contrast to single modification operation, it is not possible to add variable instructions or modify by element key. It is only possible to use the element id of the source and target. This is done asynchronously, the progress can be tracked using the batchOperationKey from the response and the batch operation status endpoint (/batch-operations/{batchOperationKey}).

completions.label: OperationReference
completions.documentation: A reference key chosen by the user that will be part of all records resulting from this operation. Must be > 0 if provided.

completions.label: PartitionsCount
completions.documentation: The number of partitions are spread across the cluster.

completions.label: PinClockAsync
completions.documentation: Pin internal clock (alpha) Set a precise, static time for the Zeebe engine's internal clock. When the clock is pinned, it remains at the specified time and does not advance. To change the time, the clock must be pinned again with a new timestamp. This endpoint is an alpha feature and may be subject to change in future releases.

completions.label: PollIntervalMs
completions.documentation: Delay (in ms) between poll cycles when no jobs are available or when at capacity. Default: 500 ms.

completions.label: PollTimeoutMs
completions.documentation: Long-poll timeout (in ms) sent to the broker. The broker holds the activation request open until jobs are available or this timeout elapses. null or 0 = broker default; negative = long polling disabled.

completions.label: ProcessDefinitionId

completions.label: ProcessDefinitionId

completions.label: ProcessDefinitionKey
completions.documentation: System-generated key for a deployed process definition.

completions.label: ProcessDefinitionKey
completions.documentation: The unique key identifying the process definition, for example, returned for a process in the deploy resources endpoint.

completions.label: ProcessDefinitionVersion
completions.documentation: The version of the process. By default, the latest version of the process is used.

completions.label: Processes
completions.documentation: Deployed process definitions.

completions.label: ProcessInstanceCreationInstruction
completions.documentation: Instructions for creating a process instance. The process definition can be specified either by id or by key.

completions.label: ProcessInstanceCreationInstructionById
completions.documentation: ProcessInstanceCreationInstructionById

completions.label: ProcessInstanceCreationInstructionByKey
completions.documentation: ProcessInstanceCreationInstructionByKey

completions.label: PublishMessageAsync

completions.label: RequestTimeout

completions.label: ResetClockAsync
completions.documentation: Reset internal clock (alpha) Resets the Zeebe engine's internal clock to the current system time, enabling it to tick in real-time. This operation is useful for returning the clock to normal behavior after it has been pinned to a specific time. This endpoint is an alpha feature and may be subject to change in future releases.

completions.label: ResolveIncidentAsync

completions.label: ResolveIncidentsBatchOperationAsync
completions.documentation: Resolve related incidents (batch) Resolves multiple instances of process instances. Since only process instances with ACTIVE state can have unresolved incidents, any given filters for state are ignored and overridden during this batch operation. This is done asynchronously, the progress can be tracked using the batchOperationKey from the response and the batch operation status endpoint (/batch-operations/{batchOperationKey}).

completions.label: ResolveProcessInstanceIncidentsAsync

completions.label: RuntimeInstructions

completions.label: RunWorkersAsync
completions.documentation: Block until cancellation is requested, keeping all registered workers alive. This is the typical entry point for worker-only applications. When the token is cancelled, all workers are stopped gracefully.

completions.documentation: Search for incidents of a specific element instance Search for incidents caused by the specified element instance, including incidents of any child instances created from this element instance. Although the `elementInstanceKey` is provided as a path parameter to indicate the root element instance, you may also include an `elementInstanceKey` within the filter object to narrow results to specific child element instances. This is useful, for example, if you want to isolate incidents associated with nested or subordinate elements within the given element instance while excluding incidents directly tied to the root element itself.

completions.label: SearchElementInstancesAsync

completions.label: SearchGlobalTaskListenersAsync

completions.label: SearchIncidentsAsync

completions.label: SearchMessageSubscriptionsAsync
completions.documentation: Search message subscriptions Search for message subscriptions based on given criteria.

completions.label: SearchProcessDefinitionsAsync
completions.documentation: Search process definitions Search for process definitions based on given criteria.

completions.label: SearchProcessInstanceIncidentsAsync
completions.documentation: Search related incidents Search for incidents caused by the process instance or any of its called process or decision instances. Although the `processInstanceKey` is provided as a path parameter to indicate the root process instance, you may also include a `processInstanceKey` within the filter object to narrow results to specific child process instances. This is useful, for example, if you want to isolate incidents associated with subprocesses or called processes under the root instance while excluding incidents directly tied to the root.

completions.documentation: Search process instances Search for process instances based on given criteria.

completions.documentation: Search roles Search for roles based on given criteria.

completions.label: SearchUsersAsync

completions.documentation: Search users for tenant Retrieves a filtered and sorted list of users for a specified tenant.

completions.label: SearchUserTaskAuditLogsAsync
completions.documentation: Search user task audit logs Search for user task audit logs based on given criteria.

completions.label: SearchUserTaskEffectiveVariablesAsync
completions.documentation: Search user task effective variables Search for the effective variables of a user task. This endpoint returns deduplicated variables where each variable name appears at most once. When the same variable name exists at multiple scope levels in the scope hierarchy, the value from the innermost scope (closest to the user task) takes precedence. This is useful for retrieving the actual runtime state of variables as seen by the user task. By default, long variable values in the response are truncated.

completions.label: SearchUserTasksAsync
completions.documentation: Search user tasks Search for user tasks based on given criteria.

completions.label: SearchUserTaskVariablesAsync
completions.documentation: Search user task variables Search for user task variables based on given criteria. This endpoint returns all variable documents visible from the user task's scope, including variables from parent scopes in the scope hierarchy. If the same variable name exists at multiple scope levels, each scope's variable is returned as a separate result. Use the `/user-tasks/{userTaskKey}/effective-variables/search` endpoint to get deduplicated variables where the innermost scope takes precedence. By default, long variable values in the response are truncated.

completions.label: SearchVariablesAsync
completions.documentation: Search variables Search for variables based on given criteria. This endpoint returns variables that exist directly at the specified scopes - it does not include variables from parent scopes that would be visible through the scope hierarchy. Variables can be process-level (scoped to the process instance) or local (scoped to specific BPMN elements like tasks, subprocesses, etc.). By default, long variable values in the response are truncated.

completions.label: SpecHash
completions.documentation: SHA-256 digest of the OpenAPI spec this SDK was generated from.

completions.label: StartInstructions
completions.documentation: List of start instructions. By default, the process instance will start at the start event. If provided, the process instance will apply start instructions after it has been created.

completions.label: StartupJitterMaxSeconds
completions.documentation: Maximum random delay (in seconds) before the worker starts polling. When multiple application instances restart simultaneously, this spreads out initial activation requests to avoid saturating the server. 0 (the default) means no delay.

completions.label: StopAllWorkersAsync
completions.documentation: Stop all registered workers and wait for in-flight jobs to drain.

completions.label: SuspendBatchOperationAsync
completions.documentation: Suspend Batch operation Suspends a running batch operation. This is done asynchronously, the progress can be tracked using the batch operation status endpoint (/batch-operations/{batchOperationKey}).

completions.label: Tags
completions.documentation: List of tags. Tags need to start with a letter; then alphanumerics, `_`, `-`, `:`, or `.`; length ≤ 100.

completions.label: TenantId
completions.documentation: The tenant id of the process definition. If multi-tenancy is enabled, provide the tenant id of the process definition to start a process instance of. If multi-tenancy is disabled, don't provide this parameter.

completions.label: TenantIds
completions.documentation: Restrict job activation to the given tenant IDs (multi-tenant setups). Cannot be combined with TenantId — setting both is rejected with ArgumentException. If neither TenantIds nor TenantId is set (or TenantIds is empty), the activation request falls back to [CamundaConfig.DefaultTenantId] (which itself defaults to "<default>" and can be overridden via the CAMUNDA_DEFAULT_TENANT_ID environment variable).

completions.label: ThrowJobErrorAsync
completions.documentation: Throw error for job Reports a business error (i.e. non-technical) that occurs while processing a job.

completions.label: TopologyResponse
completions.documentation: The response of a topology request.

completions.label: Type
completions.documentation: The job type (matches the BPMN task definition type).

completions.label: UnassignClientFromGroupAsync
completions.documentation: Unassign a client from a group Unassigns a client from a group. The client is removed as a group member, with associated authorizations, roles, and tenant assignments no longer applied.

completions.label: UnassignClientFromTenantAsync
completions.documentation: Unassign a client from a tenant Unassigns the client from the specified tenant. The client can no longer access tenant data.

completions.label: UnassignGroupFromTenantAsync
completions.documentation: Unassign a group from a tenant Unassigns a group from a specified tenant. Members of the group (users, clients) will no longer have access to the tenant's data - except they are assigned directly to the tenant.

completions.label: UnassignMappingRuleFromGroupAsync
completions.documentation: Unassign a mapping rule from a group Unassigns a mapping rule from a group.

completions.label: UnassignMappingRuleFromTenantAsync
completions.documentation: Unassign a mapping rule from a tenant Unassigns a single mapping rule from a specified tenant without deleting the rule.

completions.label: UnassignRoleFromClientAsync
completions.documentation: Unassign a role from a client Unassigns the specified role from the client. The client will no longer inherit the authorizations associated with this role.

completions.label: UnassignRoleFromGroupAsync
completions.documentation: Unassign a role from a group Unassigns the specified role from the group. All group members (user or client) no longer inherit the authorizations associated with this role.

completions.label: UnassignRoleFromMappingRuleAsync
completions.documentation: Unassign a role from a mapping rule Unassigns a role from a mapping rule.

completions.label: UnassignRoleFromTenantAsync
completions.documentation: Unassign a role from a tenant Unassigns a role from a specified tenant. Users, Clients or Groups, that have the role assigned, will no longer have access to the tenant's data - unless they are assigned directly to the tenant.

completions.label: UnassignRoleFromUserAsync
completions.documentation: Unassign a role from a user Unassigns a role from a user. The user will no longer inherit the authorizations associated with this role.

completions.label: UnassignUserFromGroupAsync
completions.documentation: Unassign a user from a group Unassigns a user from a group. The user is removed as a group member, with associated authorizations, roles, and tenant assignments no longer applied.

completions.label: UnassignUserFromTenantAsync
completions.documentation: Unassign a user from a tenant Unassigns the user from the specified tenant. The user can no longer access tenant data.

completions.label: UnassignUserTaskAsync
completions.documentation: Unassign user task Removes the assignee of a task with the given key. Unassignment waits for blocking task listeners on this lifecycle transition. If listener processing is delayed beyond the request timeout, this endpoint can return 504. Other gateway timeout causes are also possible. Retry with backoff and inspect listener worker availability and logs when this repeats.

completions.label: UpdateAuthorizationAsync
completions.documentation: Update authorization Update the authorization with the given key.

completions.label: UpdateGlobalClusterVariableAsync
completions.documentation: Update a global-scoped cluster variable Updates the value of an existing global cluster variable. The variable must exist, otherwise a 404 error is returned.

completions.label: UpdateGlobalTaskListenerAsync
completions.documentation: Update global user task listener Updates a global user task listener.

completions.label: UpdateGroupAsync

completions.label: UpdateJobAsync

completions.label: UpdateMappingRuleAsync
completions.documentation: Update mapping rule Update a mapping rule.

completions.label: UpdateRoleAsync

completions.label: UpdateTenantAsync
completions.documentation: Update tenant Updates an existing tenant.

completions.label: UpdateTenantClusterVariableAsync
completions.documentation: Update a tenant-scoped cluster variable Updates the value of an existing tenant-scoped cluster variable. The variable must exist, otherwise a 404 error is returned.

If listener processing is delayed beyond the request timeout, this endpoint can return 504. Other gateway timeout causes are also possible. Retry with backoff and inspect listener worker availability and logs when this repeats.

**`ActivateAdHocSubProcessActivitiesAsync(ElementInstanceKey, AdHocSubProcessActivateActivitiesInstruction, CancellationToken)`**

Activate activities within an ad-hoc sub-process Activates selected activities within an ad-hoc sub-process identified by element ID. The provided element IDs must exist within the ad-hoc sub-process instance identified by the provided adHocSubProcessInstanceKey.

**`class ActivatedJob`** — Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk

An activated job received from the Camunda broker, with typed variable access. This is what job handler functions receive.

**`ActivateJobsAsync(JobActivationRequest, CancellationToken)`**

Activate jobs Iterate through all known partitions and activate jobs up to the requested maximum.

**`AssignClientToGroupAsync(string, string, CancellationToken)`**

Assign a client to a group Assigns a client to a group, making it a member of the group. Members of the group inherit the group authorizations, roles, and tenant assignments.

**`AssignClientToTenantAsync(TenantId, string, CancellationToken)`**

Assign a client to a tenant Assign the client to the specified tenant. The client can then access tenant data and perform authorized actions.

**`AssignGroupToTenantAsync(TenantId, string, CancellationToken)`**

Assign a group to a tenant Assigns a group to a specified tenant. Group members (users, clients) can then access tenant data and perform authorized actions.

**`AssignMappingRuleToGroupAsync(string, string, CancellationToken)`**

Assign a mapping rule to a group Assigns a mapping rule to a group.

**`AssignMappingRuleToTenantAsync(TenantId, string, CancellationToken)`**

Assign a mapping rule to a tenant Assign a single mapping rule to a specified tenant.

**`AssignRoleToClientAsync(string, string, CancellationToken)`**

Assign a role to a client Assigns the specified role to the client. The client will inherit the authorizations associated with this role.

**`AssignRoleToGroupAsync(string, string, CancellationToken)`**

Assign a role to a group Assigns the specified role to the group. Every member of the group (user or client) will inherit the authorizations associated with this role.

**`AssignRoleToMappingRuleAsync(string, string, CancellationToken)`**

Assign a role to a mapping rule Assigns a role to a mapping rule.

**`AssignRoleToTenantAsync(TenantId, string, CancellationToken)`**

Assign a role to a tenant Assigns a role to a specified tenant. Users, Clients or Groups, that have the role assigned, will get access to the tenant's data and can perform actions according to their authorizations.

**`AssignRoleToUserAsync(string, Username, CancellationToken)`**

Assign a role to a user Assigns the specified role to the user. The user will inherit the authorizations associated with this role.

**`AssignUserTaskAsync(UserTaskKey, UserTaskAssignmentRequest, CancellationToken)`**

Assign user task Assigns a user task with the given key to the given assignee. Assignment waits for blocking task listeners on this lifecycle transition. If listener processing is delayed beyond the request timeout, this endpoint can return 504. Other gateway timeout causes are also possible. Retry with backoff and inspect listener worker availability and logs when this repeats.

**`AssignUserToGroupAsync(string, Username, CancellationToken)`**

Assign a user to a group Assigns a user to a group, making the user a member of the group. Group members inherit the group authorizations, roles, and tenant assignments.

**`AssignUserToTenantAsync(TenantId, Username, CancellationToken)`**

Assign a user to a tenant Assign a single user to a specified tenant. The user can then access tenant data and perform authorized actions.

**`AssumeExists(string)`**

Creates a ProcessDefinitionId from a raw string value. Use this when side-loading values not received from an API call.

---

**`AssumeExists(string)`**

Creates a ProcessDefinitionKey from a raw string value. Use this when side-loading values not received from an API call.

**`JobWorkerConfig.AutoStart`**

Whether to start polling immediately on creation. Default: true .

**`ProcessInstanceCreationInstructionById.AwaitCompletion`**

Wait for the process instance to complete. If the process instance does not complete within the request timeout limit, a 504 response status will be returned. The process instance will continue to run in the background regardless of the timeout. Disabled by default.

---

**`ProcessInstanceCreationInstructionByKey.AwaitCompletion`**

Wait for the process instance to complete. If the process instance does not complete within the request timeout limit, a 504 response status will be returned. The process instance will continue to run in the background regardless of the timeout. Disabled by default.

**`BroadcastSignalAsync(SignalBroadcastRequest, CancellationToken)`**

Broadcast signal Broadcasts a signal.

**`TopologyResponse.Brokers`**

A list of brokers that are part of this cluster.

**`ProcessInstanceCreationInstructionById.BusinessId`**

An optional, user-defined string identifier that identifies the process instance within the scope of a process definition (scoped by tenant). If provided and uniqueness enforcement is enabled, the engine will reject creation if another root process instance with the same business id is already active for the same process definition. Note that any active child process instances with the same business id are not taken into account.

---

**`ProcessInstanceCreationInstructionByKey.BusinessId`**

An optional, user-defined string identifier that identifies the process instance within the scope of a process definition (scoped by tenant). If provided and uniqueness enforcement is enabled, the engine will reject creation if another root process instance with the same business id is already active for the same process definition. Note that any active child process instances with the same business id are not taken into account.

**`class CamundaClient`** — Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk

Primary Camunda client. Provides typed methods for all Camunda 8 REST API operations. Auto-generated operation methods are added in the Generated/ partial class files. This class provides the infrastructure: configuration, auth, retry, backpressure.

---

**`new CamundaClient(CamundaOptions)`**

Create a new CamundaClient with the given options.

**`class CamundaOptions`** — Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk

Options for constructing a CamundaClient. Mirrors the JS SDK's CamundaOptions with idiomatic C# conventions.

**`CancelBatchOperationAsync(BatchOperationKey, CancellationToken)`**

Cancel Batch operation Cancels a running batch operation. This is done asynchronously, the progress can be tracked using the batch operation status endpoint (/batch-operations/{batchOperationKey}).

**`CancelProcessInstanceAsync(ProcessInstanceKey, CancelProcessInstanceRequest, CancellationToken)`**

Cancel process instance Cancels a running process instance. As a cancellation includes more than just the removal of the process instance resource, the cancellation resource must be posted. Cancellation can wait on listener-related processing; when that processing does not complete in time, this endpoint can return 504. Other gateway timeout causes are also possible. Retry with backoff and inspect listener worker availability and logs when this repeats.

**`CancelProcessInstancesBatchOperationAsync(ProcessInstanceCancellationBatchOperationRequest, CancellationToken)`**

Cancel process instances (batch) Cancels multiple running process instances. Since only ACTIVE root instances can be cancelled, any given filters for state and parentProcessInstanceKey are ignored and overridden during this batch operation. This is done asynchronously, the progress can be tracked using the batchOperationKey from the response and the batch operation status endpoint (/batch-operations/{batchOperationKey}).

**`TopologyResponse.ClusterId`**

The cluster Id.

**`TopologyResponse.ClusterSize`**

The number of brokers in the cluster.

**`CompleteJobAsync(JobKey, JobCompletionRequest, CancellationToken)`**

Complete job Complete a job with the given payload, which allows completing the associated service task.

**`CompleteUserTaskAsync(UserTaskKey, UserTaskCompletionRequest, CancellationToken)`**

Complete user task Completes a user task with the given key. Completion waits for blocking task listeners on this lifecycle transition. If listener processing is delayed beyond the request timeout, this endpoint can return 504. Other gateway timeout causes are also possible. Retry with backoff and inspect listener worker availability and logs when this repeats.

**`CamundaClient.Config`**

The current hydrated configuration (read-only).

---

**`CamundaOptions.Config`**

Strongly typed env-style overrides (CAMUNDA_* keys).

**`CamundaOptions.Configuration`**

An IConfiguration section (typically configuration.GetSection("Camunda") ) to bind settings from appsettings.json or any other configuration provider. Keys use PascalCase property names (e.g. RestAddress , Auth:Strategy ) and are mapped to the canonical CAMUNDA_* env-var names internally. Precedence (highest wins): Config > Configuration > environment variables > defaults.

**`CorrelateMessageAsync(MessageCorrelationRequest, CancellationToken)`**

Correlate message Publishes a message and correlates it to a subscription. If correlation is successful it will return the first process instance key the message correlated with. The message is not buffered. Use the publish message endpoint to send messages that can be buffered.

**`Create(CamundaOptions)`**

Create a new CamundaClient.

**`CreateAdminUserAsync(UserRequest, CancellationToken)`**

Create admin user Creates a new user and assigns the admin role to it. This endpoint is only usable when users are managed in the Orchestration Cluster and while no user is assigned to the admin role.

**`CreateAuthorizationAsync(AuthorizationRequest, CancellationToken)`**

Create authorization Create the authorization.

**`CreateDeploymentAsync(MultipartFormDataContent, CancellationToken)`**

Deploy resources Deploys one or more resources (e.g. processes, decision models, or forms). This is an atomic call, i.e. either all resources are deployed or none of them are.

**`CreateDocumentAsync(MultipartFormDataContent, string, DocumentId?, CancellationToken)`**

Upload document Upload a document to the Camunda 8 cluster. Note that this is currently supported for document stores of type: AWS, GCP, in-memory (non-production), local (non-production)

**`CreateDocumentLinkAsync(DocumentId, DocumentLinkRequest, string, string, CancellationToken)`**

Create document link Create a link to a document in the Camunda 8 cluster. Note that this is currently supported for document stores of type: AWS, GCP

**`CreateDocumentsAsync(MultipartFormDataContent, string, CancellationToken)`**

Upload multiple documents Upload multiple documents to the Camunda 8 cluster. The caller must provide a file name for each document, which will be used in case of a multi-status response to identify which documents failed to upload. The file name can be provided in the `Content-Disposition` header of the file part or in the `fileName` field of the metadata. You can add a parallel array of metadata objects. These are matched with the files based on index, and must have the same length as the files array. To pass homogenous metadata for all files, spread the metadata over the metadata array. A filename value provided explicitly via the metadata array in the request overrides the `Content-Disposition` header of the file part. In case of a multi-status response, the response body will contain a list of `DocumentBatchProblemDetail` objects, each of which contains the file name of the document that failed to upload and the reason for the failure. The client can choose to retry the whole batch or individual documents based on the response. Note that this is currently supported for document stores of type: AWS, GCP, in-memory (non-production), local (non-production)

**`CreateElementInstanceVariablesAsync(ElementInstanceKey, SetVariableRequest, CancellationToken)`**

Update element instance variables Updates all the variables of a particular scope (for example, process instance, element instance) with the given variable data. Specify the element instance in the `elementInstanceKey` parameter. Variable updates can be delayed by listener-related processing; if processing exceeds the request timeout, this endpoint can return 504. Other gateway timeout causes are also possible. Retry with backoff and inspect listener worker availability and logs when this repeats.

**`CreateGlobalClusterVariableAsync(CreateClusterVariableRequest, CancellationToken)`**

Create a global-scoped cluster variable Create a global-scoped cluster variable.

**`CreateGlobalTaskListenerAsync(CreateGlobalTaskListenerRequest, CancellationToken)`**

Create global user task listener Create a new global user task listener.

**`CreateGroupAsync(GroupCreateRequest, CancellationToken)`**

Create group Create a new group.

**`CreateJobWorker(JobWorkerConfig config, JobHandler handler)`**

Create a job worker that polls for and processes jobs of the specified type. The handler receives an ActivatedJob and returns variables to auto-complete. Throw BpmnErrorException for BPMN errors, JobFailureException for explicit failures, or any other exception to auto-fail with retries - 1 .

*Returns:* The running JobWorker instance.

---

**`CreateJobWorker(JobWorkerConfig, Func<ActivatedJob, CancellationToken, Task>)`**

Create a job worker with a handler that doesn't return output variables. The job is auto-completed with no variables on success.

**`CreateMappingRuleAsync(MappingRuleCreateRequest, CancellationToken)`**

Create mapping rule Create a new mapping rule

**`CreateProcessInstanceAsync(ProcessInstanceCreationInstruction, CancellationToken)`**

Create process instance Creates and starts an instance of the specified process. The process definition to use to create the instance can be specified either using its unique key (as returned by Deploy resources), or using the BPMN process id and a version. Waits for the completion of the process instance before returning a result when awaitCompletion is enabled.

**`CreateRoleAsync(RoleCreateRequest, CancellationToken)`**

Create role Create a new role.

**`CreateTenantAsync(TenantCreateRequest, CancellationToken)`**

Create tenant Creates a new tenant.

**`CreateTenantClusterVariableAsync(TenantId, CreateClusterVariableRequest, CancellationToken)`**

Create a tenant-scoped cluster variable Create a new cluster variable for the given tenant.

**`CreateUserAsync(UserRequest, CancellationToken)`**

Create user Create a new user.

Raw custom headers (typically a JsonElement at runtime).

UNIX epoch timestamp (ms) when the job lock expires.

Deployed decision requirements.

**`ExtendedDeploymentResponse.Decisions`**

Deployed decision definitions.

**`DeleteAuthorizationAsync(AuthorizationKey, CancellationToken)`**

**`DeleteDecisionInstanceAsync(DecisionEvaluationKey, DeleteDecisionInstanceRequest, CancellationToken)`**

Delete decision instance Delete all associated decision evaluations based on provided key.

**`DeleteDecisionInstancesBatchOperationAsync(DecisionInstanceDeletionBatchOperationRequest, CancellationToken)`**

Delete decision instances (batch) Delete multiple decision instances. This will delete the historic data from secondary storage. This is done asynchronously, the progress can be tracked using the batchOperationKey from the response and the batch operation status endpoint (/batch-operations/{batchOperationKey}).

**`DeleteDocumentAsync(DocumentId, string, CancellationToken)`**

Delete document Delete a document from the Camunda 8 cluster. Note that this is currently supported for document stores of type: AWS, GCP, in-memory (non-production), local (non-production)

Delete resource Deletes a deployed resource. This can be a process definition, decision requirements definition, or form definition deployed using the deploy resources endpoint. Specify the resource you want to delete in the `resourceKey` parameter. Once a resource has been deleted it cannot be recovered. If the resource needs to be available again, a new deployment of the resource is required. By default, only the resource itself is deleted from the runtime state. To also delete the historic data associated with a resource, set the `deleteHistory` flag in the request body to `true`. The historic data is deleted asynchronously via a batch operation. The details of the created batch operation are included in the response. Note that history deletion is only supported for process resources; for other resource types this flag is ignored and no history will be deleted.

**`JobWorkerConfig.MaxConcurrentJobs`**

Maximum number of jobs that may be in-flight (activated and being handled) concurrently by this worker. Controls how many jobs are requested per poll and how many handler tasks run in parallel. For I/O-bound handlers (HTTP calls, database queries), higher values (32–128) improve throughput because async handlers release threads during awaits. For CPU-bound handlers, set to ProcessorCount or lower to avoid over-subscribing the thread pool. Set to 1 for sequential (single-job-at-a-time) processing. Falls back to CAMUNDA_WORKER_MAX_CONCURRENT_JOBS environment variable, then 10 .

**`MigrateProcessInstanceAsync(ProcessInstanceKey, ProcessInstanceMigrationInstruction, CancellationToken)`**

Migrate process instance Migrates a process instance to a new process definition. This request can contain multiple mapping instructions to define mapping between the active process instance's elements and target process definition elements. Use this to upgrade a process instance to a new version of a process or to a different process definition, e.g. to keep your running instances up-to-date with the latest process improvements.

**`MigrateProcessInstancesBatchOperationAsync(ProcessInstanceMigrationBatchOperationRequest, CancellationToken)`**

Migrate process instances (batch) Migrate multiple process instances. Since only process instances with ACTIVE state can be migrated, any given filters for state are ignored and overridden during this batch operation. This is done asynchronously, the progress can be tracked using the batchOperationKey from the response and the batch operation status endpoint (/batch-operations/{batchOperationKey}).

**`ModifyProcessInstanceAsync(ProcessInstanceKey, ProcessInstanceModificationInstruction, CancellationToken)`**

Modify process instance Modifies a running process instance. This request can contain multiple instructions to activate an element of the process or to terminate an active instance of an element. Use this to repair a process instance that is stuck on an element or took an unintended path. For example, because an external system is not available or doesn't respond as expected.

**`ModifyProcessInstancesBatchOperationAsync(ProcessInstanceModificationBatchOperationRequest, CancellationToken)`**

Modify process instances (batch) Modify multiple process instances. Since only process instances with ACTIVE state can be modified, any given filters for state are ignored and overridden during this batch operation. In contrast to single modification operation, it is not possible to add variable instructions or modify by element key. It is only possible to use the element id of the source and target. This is done asynchronously, the progress can be tracked using the batchOperationKey from the response and the batch operation status endpoint (/batch-operations/{batchOperationKey}).

Use the `/user-tasks/{userTaskKey}/effective-variables/search` endpoint to get deduplicated variables where the innermost scope takes precedence. signatures.label: CreateDocumentsAsync(MultipartFormDataContent, string, CancellationToken)
signatures.documentation: Upload multiple documents Upload multiple documents to the Camunda 8 cluster. The caller must provide a file name for each document, which will be used in case of a multi-status response to identify which documents failed to upload. The file name can be provided in the `Content-Disposition` header of the file part or in the `fileName` field of the metadata. You can add a parallel array of metadata objects. These are matched with the files based on index, and must have the same length as the files array. To pass homogenous metadata for all files, spread the metadata over the metadata array. A filename value provided explicitly via the metadata array in the request overrides the `Content-Disposition` header of the file part. In case of a multi-status response, the response body will contain a list of `DocumentBatchProblemDetail` objects, each of which contains the file name of the document that failed to upload and the reason for the failure. The client can choose to retry the whole batch or individual documents based on the response. Note that this is currently supported for document stores of type: AWS, GCP, in-memory (non-production), local (non-production)

If the resource needs to be available again, a new deployment of the resource is required. By default, only the resource itself is deleted from the runtime state. signatures.label: MigrateProcessInstanceAsync(ProcessInstanceKey, ProcessInstanceMigrationInstruction, CancellationToken)
signatures.documentation: Migrate process instance Migrates a process instance to a new process definition. This request can contain multiple mapping instructions to define mapping between the active process instance's elements and target process definition elements. Use this to upgrade a process instance to a new version of a process or to a different process definition, e.g. to keep your running instances up-to-date with the latest process improvements.

signatures.label: MigrateProcessInstancesBatchOperationAsync(ProcessInstanceMigrationBatchOperationRequest, CancellationToken)
signatures.documentation: Migrate process instances (batch) Migrate multiple process instances. Since only process instances with ACTIVE state can be migrated, any given filters for state are ignored and overridden during this batch operation. This is done asynchronously, the progress can be tracked using the batchOperationKey from the response and the batch operation status endpoint (/batch-operations/{batchOperationKey}).

signatures.label: ModifyProcessInstanceAsync(ProcessInstanceKey, ProcessInstanceModificationInstruction, CancellationToken)
signatures.documentation: Modify process instance Modifies a running process instance. This request can contain multiple instructions to activate an element of the process or to terminate an active instance of an element. Use this to repair a process instance that is stuck on an element or took an unintended path. For example, because an external system is not available or doesn't respond as expected.

signatures.label: ModifyProcessInstancesBatchOperationAsync(ProcessInstanceModificationBatchOperationRequest, CancellationToken)
signatures.documentation: Modify process instances (batch) Modify multiple process instances. Since only process instances with ACTIVE state can be modified, any given filters for state are ignored and overridden during this batch operation. In contrast to single modification operation, it is not possible to add variable instructions or modify by element key. It is only possible to use the element id of the source and target. This is done asynchronously, the progress can be tracked using the batchOperationKey from the response and the batch operation status endpoint (/batch-operations/{batchOperationKey}).

signatures.label: PinClockAsync(ClockPinRequest, CancellationToken)
signatures.documentation: Pin internal clock (alpha) Set a precise, static time for the Zeebe engine's internal clock. When the clock is pinned, it remains at the specified time and does not advance. To change the time, the clock must be pinned again with a new timestamp. This endpoint is an alpha feature and may be subject to change in future releases.

signatures.label: PublishMessageAsync(MessagePublicationRequest, CancellationToken)
signatures.documentation: Publish message Publishes a single message. Messages are published to specific partitions computed from their correlation keys. Messages can be buffered. The endpoint does not wait for a correlation result. Use the message correlation endpoint for such use cases.

signatures.label: ResetClockAsync(CancellationToken)
signatures.documentation: Reset internal clock (alpha) Resets the Zeebe engine's internal clock to the current system time, enabling it to tick in real-time. This operation is useful for returning the clock to normal behavior after it has been pinned to a specific time. This endpoint is an alpha feature and may be subject to change in future releases.

signatures.label: ResolveIncidentAsync(IncidentKey, IncidentResolutionRequest, CancellationToken)
signatures.documentation: Resolve incident Marks the incident as resolved; most likely a call to Update job will be necessary to reset the job's retries, followed by this call.

signatures.label: ResolveIncidentsBatchOperationAsync(ProcessInstanceIncidentResolutionBatchOperationRequest, CancellationToken)
signatures.documentation: Resolve related incidents (batch) Resolves multiple instances of process instances. Since only process instances with ACTIVE state can have unresolved incidents, any given filters for state are ignored and overridden during this batch operation. This is done asynchronously, the progress can be tracked using the batchOperationKey from the response and the batch operation status endpoint (/batch-operations/{batchOperationKey}).

signatures.label: ResolveProcessInstanceIncidentsAsync(ProcessInstanceKey, CancellationToken)
signatures.documentation: Resolve related incidents Creates a batch operation to resolve multiple incidents of a process instance.

signatures.label: ResumeBatchOperationAsync(BatchOperationKey, CancellationToken)
signatures.documentation: Resume Batch operation Resumes a suspended batch operation. This is done asynchronously, the progress can be tracked using the batch operation status endpoint (/batch-operations/{batchOperationKey}).

signatures.label: RunWorkersAsync(TimeSpan? ct, CancellationToken gracePeriod)
signatures.documentation: Block until cancellation is requested, keeping all registered workers alive. This is the typical entry point for worker-only applications. When the token is cancelled, all workers are stopped gracefully.

parameters.label: CancellationToken gracePeriod
parameters.documentation: Time to wait for in-flight jobs to finish during shutdown. Default: 10 seconds.

signatures.label: SearchUserTaskEffectiveVariablesAsync(UserTaskKey, SearchUserTaskEffectiveVariablesRequest, bool?, ConsistencyOptions<VariableSearchQueryResult>, CancellationToken)
signatures.documentation: Search user task effective variables Search for the effective variables of a user task. This endpoint returns deduplicated variables where each variable name appears at most once. When the same variable name exists at multiple scope levels in the scope hierarchy, the value from the innermost scope (closest to the user task) takes precedence. This is useful for retrieving the actual runtime state of variables as seen by the user task. By default, long variable values in the response are truncated.

signatures.label: SearchUserTasksAsync(UserTaskSearchQuery, ConsistencyOptions<UserTaskSearchQueryResult>, CancellationToken)
signatures.documentation: Search user tasks Search for user tasks based on given criteria.

signatures.label: SearchUserTaskVariablesAsync(UserTaskKey, SearchUserTaskVariablesRequest, bool?, ConsistencyOptions<VariableSearchQueryResult>, CancellationToken)
signatures.documentation: Search user task variables Search for user task variables based on given criteria. This endpoint returns all variable documents visible from the user task's scope, including variables from parent scopes in the scope hierarchy. If the same variable name exists at multiple scope levels, each scope's variable is returned as a separate result. Use the `/user-tasks/{userTaskKey}/effective-variables/search` endpoint to get deduplicated variables where the innermost scope takes precedence. By default, long variable values in the response are truncated.

signatures.label: SearchVariablesAsync(SearchVariablesRequest, bool?, ConsistencyOptions<VariableSearchQueryResult>, CancellationToken)
signatures.documentation: Search variables Search for variables based on given criteria. This endpoint returns variables that exist directly at the specified scopes - it does not include variables from parent scopes that would be visible through the scope hierarchy. Variables can be process-level (scoped to the process instance) or local (scoped to specific BPMN elements like tasks, subprocesses, etc.). By default, long variable values in the response are truncated.
